title: Fire service officers (watch manager and below)
description: Plan, direct, or coordinate entertainment and recreational activities and operations of a recreational facility, including cruise ships and parks. All personal service managers not listed separately. Manage or coordinate fitness and wellness programs and services. Manage and train staff of wellness specialists, health educators, or fitness instructors. (Based on 19 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Rescue survivors from burning buildings, accident sites, and water hazards.
Maintain knowledge of current firefighting practices by participating in drills, seminars, conventions, and conferences.
Dress with equipment such as fire-resistant clothing and breathing apparatus before responding to emergency situations.
Assess fires and situations, report conditions to superiors, and maintain contact with fire dispatchers for additional resources.
Respond to fire alarms and other assistance calls, including automobile and industrial accidents.
Create openings in buildings for ventilation or entrance, and extinguish flames and embers using appropriate tools and techniques.
Drive and operate firefighting vehicles and equipment while collaborating with other firefighters as a team.
Inspect fire sites after flames are extinguished to ensure there is no further danger, and patrol burned areas to eliminate hot spots.
Position and climb ladders to gain access to upper levels of buildings or rescue individuals from burning structures.
Collaborate with police and other agencies to respond to accidents, disasters, and investigate potential arson cases.
Prepare written reports detailing fire incidents, inspections performed, fire code violations, and corrective recommendations offered.
Inspect buildings for fire hazards, compliance with fire prevention ordinances, and test smoke alarms and fire suppression equipment as necessary.
Participate in physical training activities to maintain high levels of fitness, and teach public education programs on fire safety and prevention.
Recommend changes to fire prevention practices, procedures, and fire code enforcement as necessary.
Conduct fire exit drills to evaluate evacuation procedures and develop or review fire exit plans.
Photograph evidence from fire sites and submit reports for investigations, including determining the cause of a fire or explosion.
Coordinate training for new employees on fire control and suppression techniques, and ensure they understand safety regulations.
Review blueprints and plans for compliance with fire safety codes before the construction of new buildings or renovations.
Conduct inspections on newly installed fire protection systems to verify compliance with legal and safety standards.
Inspect fire protection or detection systems and respond to maintenance issues of firefighting equipment when necessary.
Direct investigation of cases of suspected arson, hazards, and false alarms, and submit reports outlining findings.
Maintain required maps and records for fire operations.
Recommend equipment modifications or new equipment purchases, and perform maintenance and minor repairs on firefighting equipment.
Drive crew carriers to transport firefighters to fire sites, and communicate fire details using two-way radios.
Instruct and drill fire department personnel in assigned duties including firefighting, medical care, and hazardous materials response.
Participate in creating fire safety guidelines and evacuation schemes for nonresidential buildings, and supervise property inspections for compliance with fire codes.
Evaluate performance of assigned firefighting personnel and prepare management reports regarding compliance operations and progress.
Schedule employee work assignments, set work priorities, and assign tasks to staff members.
Provide emergency medical services as required and perform light to heavy rescue functions at emergencies.
Inspect and test new and existing fire protection systems and equipment to ensure proper operation.
Monitor fire suppression expenditures to ensure necessity and reasonableness.
Serve as a working leader of a crew of three or more firefighters while conducting fire suppression activities.
Assess the nature and extent of fire, building conditions, and danger to adjacent buildings to determine crew needs.
Conduct internal reviews or audits to ensure compliance with applicable fire safety standards and regulations.
Train and supervise fitness or wellness specialists, and develop or coordinate wellness programs.
Respond to customer inquiries or complaints about fire services or safety information.
Monitor operations to ensure compliance with health, safety, or hygiene standards in firefighting practices.
Identify environmental contamination sources and assess environmental damage or monitor cleanup progress.
Design or implement plans for structural demolition, surface or ground water remediation, and debris removal.
Develop or implement plans for revegetation of brownfield sites and direct brownfield redevelopment projects for safety and compliance.
Inspect sites to recommend improvements, maintain records, and prepare reports related to environmental redevelopment projects.
Negotiate contracts for services or materials needed for environmental remediation and prepare permit applications for projects.
Train staff in regulatory policies or hazardous material cleanup procedures, and conduct training on wellness programs, including CPR.
Develop relationships with law enforcement or environmental regulatory agencies to investigate theft or compliance issues.
Coordinate internal investigations of theft and maintain documentation of loss prevention activities.
Evaluate fitness and wellness programs to track effectiveness and assess strategies for loss reduction and compliance monitoring.
Keep informed of industry changes, monitor regulatory affairs related to environmental issues, and propose modifications to compliance practices.
